summary:
A Nance space maintainer is recommended due to its ability to preserve proper spacing without altering jaw development, minimizing risks associated with more invasive procedures that could disrupt eruptive patterns of incoming permanent teeth. This choice supports long-term dental health while accommodating normal growth processes typical at age seven. To ensure brevity:

A Nance space maintainer preserves optimal tooth spacing post-extraction without risking adverse effects on developing dentition, supporting healthy oral development as the child grows. Given no indication suggests aggressive intervention was needed beyond basic preservation techniques, this method remains both safe and effective for guiding subsequent eruptions correctly.
